content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer sequence AAA with length NNN.

Find the number of (unordered) pairs of elements such that the average of these two elements is also present in the sequence. Formally, find the number of pairs (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) such that 1≤i<j≤N1 \le i \lt j \le N1≤i<j≤N and there is an index kkk (1≤k≤N1 \le k \le N1≤k≤N) for which 2Ak=Ai+Aj2A_k = A_i+A_j2Ak​=Ai​+Aj​ holds.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of valid pairs (i, j).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: Example case 1: We can take k for Ak to be either 1 or 2, since Ak = 2 either way. Since the problem asks for unordered pairs, (1, 2) is the only possible valid pair (the same as (2, 1)). Hence, the answer is 1. Example case 2: We see that A1 = 2 = (1 + 3) / 2. No other valid pair exists.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a hashing approach. First, we sort the array and then for each pair of elements, we calculate their average and check if it exists in the array using a hash set. We count the number of such pairs and return the count.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^2) — explain: per query is O(N^2), total is O(T * N^2)
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — explain: we need to store the array and the hash set
